TextENGNew: …Before I knew it, the cicadas had yielded their chorus to the higurashi.
That chorus welled up from far away, swelled, then faded—swelled, then faded. It was so fragile, so ephemeral.
And Rena's voice was much the same—like the higurashi's cry, it rose and then dissolved, rose and dissolved.
…She seemed so fragile that I feared the slightest distraction might cause even her presence to vanish into the air.
changeReason: Original focuses on sound/words vanishing, while new translation captures the Japanese (その姿さえも掻き消えてしまう) meaning her very presence/figure might vanish, not just her voice.

TextENGNew: But no one rushed her.
Mion dusted off the hood of an abandoned car and settled onto it, striking a posture that broadcast her ease.
Her expression was soft, as though she had always intended to come here for a quiet evening in the cool air.
Watching her, Satoko found her own spot and sat down as well.
Satoko, however, didn't look nearly as relaxed.
I thought about following suit, but even the few seconds it would take to search for somewhere to sit felt like a rudeness toward Rena, so I stayed standing.
Rika-chan didn't sit either.
She wasn't looking at Rena.
Everyone else had their eyes on Rena—but Rika-chan alone looked up, filling her gaze with the high sky that was just beginning to blush with crimson.
changeReason: New translation captures 'high sky beginning to blush with crimson' (朱が混じり始めている高い空), while old just says 'red sky' — losing the nuance of the sky only starting to turn.

TextENGNew: Her expression was… difficult to describe.
It was utterly desolate—as though every shade of joy and anger, sorrow and pleasure had been drained away, leaving only what remained afterward.
changeReason: New translation faithfully renders the Japanese detail about specific emotions (喜怒, 哀楽) being drained, while the original reduces this to 'pale and blank.'

TextENGNew: And of all of us, that expression most closely resembled Rena's.
changeReason: Original says 'Rena wore the same expression' (implying Rena's face is being described), but the Japanese says Rika's expression most resembled Rena's — the subject is different.

TextENGNew: Rena kept trying to form words, only to have them catch in her throat again and again.
…When she noticed Rika-chan gazing at the sky, she tilted her own face upward and let the wide heavens fill her eyes.
changeReason: New translation captures the vivid Japanese imagery (瞳いっぱいに大空を映しこむ — filling her eyes with the vast sky) that the original reduced to simply 'decided to look that way.'

TextENGNew: Seeing that, Mion looked up too.
…And I did as well.
Satoko followed everyone's lead and looked up.
And then—silence.
We stood inside a concert hall filled by nothing but the higurashi's chorus—the concert hall with the highest ceiling in the world.
…A cool breeze drifted through and tickled the faint sweat the day's heat had drawn out. It felt wonderful.
changeReason: New translation adds 'It felt wonderful' (気持ち良かった) and specifies 'faint sweat' (薄い汗), both from the Japanese, which the original omitted.

TextENGNew: Standing like this, I almost let myself believe we had all gathered here simply to enjoy a pleasant evening breeze.
More than that—I wanted to suggest we pretend that, even now.
changeReason: Original says 'I could only wish' (passive longing) while the Japanese (提案すらしたくなった) means 'I even wanted to suggest it' — the new captures the active desire to propose pretending.

TextENGNew: Only the shadows of birds crossing the sky occasionally marred our quiet concert with their gracelessness.
…Because each time I became aware of a bird in flight, I was reminded that time had not stopped.
None of us wanted to hurry Rena.
And so we could not even allow the ticking of the clock to rush her.
We wanted to hold that cool, evening sky in place—suspended in endless time—until Rena could settle her heart and speak.
Just then, a dull, hollow thunk brought us back to reality.
changeReason: New translation renders the onomatopoeia 'ボコン' as 'a dull, hollow thunk' while the original just says 'a sound,' losing the characterization of the noise.

TextENGNew: Mion had shifted her legs, and the hood she was sitting on had made the sound.
Whether she'd done it on purpose or by accident I couldn't say—but that sound, cruelly, reminded us that time had been passing all along.
changeReason: New translation includes 'cruelly' (残酷にも) from the Japanese, an important adverb the original omitted.

TextENGNew: The graceless noise drew our gazes from the sky and returned them to earth.
That inelegant sound seemed to have given Rena the push she needed to summon her resolve.
changeReason: Original says 'urged Rena to speak' while the Japanese (覚悟を促す) means 'prompted her resolve' — the new translation captures that it was about steeling herself, not just speaking.

TextENGNew: Rena let out a small breath, bowed her head slightly as if swallowing something, and then looked up at us with the expression we all knew so well.
changeReason: Original says 'nodded as if she understood something' while the Japanese (何かを飲み込むように俯く) describes bowing her head as if swallowing something — a different gesture and meaning.

TextENGNew: "…Before I start, there's… one thing I want to say."
She was probably saying it to everyone, but since she was looking at me—standing at the center of the group—I nodded on everyone's behalf.
"…I believe I tried my best, in my own way."
changeReason: Original says 'I did the right thing' while the Japanese (正しい努力をした) means 'I made the right effort/tried properly' — different emphasis between outcome and process.

TextENGNew: Her voice was no longer ragged.
It held the same quiet clarity it always did—the same composure.
changeReason: New translation captures the specific quality described in the Japanese (毅然とした響き — a resolute ring/composure) while the original just says 'sounded just like she always did.'

TextENGNew: "I wonder… how much effort a person is allowed to make in order to be happy…"
It sounded like a question, but no one answered.
"That's why I love Mii-chan's club. The idea that you do absolutely whatever it takes to win—I think that's beautiful."

"…Because if the world only allowed you to fight by the rules—if there were a strict limit on the kind of effort you could make—then people who aren't happy would never be able to win happiness at all, would they?"
"Misfortune is something that chains together, I think. Once it begins, it's very hard to escape. And luck or someone else's help—neither of those alone is anywhere near enough to break free."
"I think you have to struggle yourself—more and more, with everything you have. I believe that's the only way to finally grasp… something like happiness."
Rena paused there, looked up at the sky, took a breath, and opened her mouth again.
"But… I don't expect you to accept what I did, either."
"…Because it really was filthy. You were all just now—stunned, weren't you? That says everything…"
changeReason: Original says 'a very bad thing' while the Japanese (汚らしい) means 'filthy/dirty' — a more visceral, self-loathing characterization that the new translation preserves.

TextENGNew: I hadn't known Rena was capable of smiling like that—so full of self-contempt.
That smile was painful to look at beyond belief.
…And it made me feel a distance that had no right to exist.
There was nothing between us—no barrier of any kind—and yet it felt as though she were speaking to us through iron bars, or chain-link fencing. That's how vast the gulf was.
"…But that's fine. There's nothing to be done about it. That's just how the world is made."
"If I were the one in your position… I'm sure I would have looked just as stunned, and said something irresponsible, something without mercy."
Those words were probably Rena's only counterattack against us.
We were spectators wearing sympathy like a mask, watching a clown perform on a stage and calling it someone else's problem. That single remark was her only riposte.
changeReason: New translation adds 'calling it someone else's problem' (他人事) and specifies 'single remark was her only riposte' (たった一つだけの反撃), details from the Japanese the original omitted.

TextENGNew: I wonder if she expected one of us to say something back.
…Of course, not one of us could find a word to offer.
Rena looked disappointed for just a moment—and then, with effortless lightness, she climbed atop an abandoned car and stepped onto the roof as though it were a stage.
changeReason: New translation includes 'stepped onto the roof as though it were a stage' (屋根の上のステージに乗った) and 'effortless lightness' (軽やかに), details from the Japanese the original simplified.

TextENGNew: She spread both arms wide, spun lightly so her skirt swirled out around her, then caught its hem between her fingertips and gave us an elegant, theatrical bow.
"Well then… I'll begin. The story of Ryuugu Rena's one great endeavor."
changeReason: Old adds 'are you ready for a story?' not in Japanese, and translates 一世一代 as just 'her best' rather than 'one great endeavor.'

TextENGNew: There was no curtain, and no spotlight.
Her stage was the roof of a wrecked car amid the trash heaps.
We resolved to miss not a single word of whatever she was about to say.
That was the one thing we could do—the one thing we believed could not hurt her.
